lines.en_lit: And Rabbi Yehuda does not disagree.
Abaye said: Since the Master, said: implied precludes implied implied on their own disagrees
“And the pure one shall sprinkle upon the impure” pure, by inference he was ritually impure teaches about one who immersed that day, that he is qualified in heifer.

Rabbi Asi said: When Rabbi Yoḥanan and Reish Lakish analyzed of the heifer They brought up from it only as the amount that the fox brings up from a plowed field, Rather, they said that implied preclude implied implied on their own

A tanna recited before Rabbi Yoḥanan: All slaughterings are valid by a non-priest, except that of heifer. Rabbi Yoḥanan said to him: Go out and teach outside We have not found a slaughtering by a non-priest invalid.

And Rabbi Yoḥanan Needless to say that he did not listen to tanna, but he did not even listen to his teacher, as Rabbi Yoḥanan said in the name of Rabbi Shimon ben Yehotzadak: The slaughtering of the heifer by a non-priest is invalid. And I say it is valid, we have not found a slaughtering by a non-priest invalid.

comes next to his bull a second What is different about the first confession which he did not say: And the children of Aaron, your sacred people, and what is different about the second confession which he said: And the children of Aaron, your sacred people?

The school of Rabbi Yishmael taught: That is the attribute of justice lends Better that an innocent person should come and gain atonement on behalf of the guilty, and a guilty person should not come and gain atonement on behalf of guilty person.

MISHNA: would slaughter and receive its blood in a bowl, and give it to the one who stirs it. on the fourth row in the Sanctuary lest it coagulate He would take a coal pan and ascend to the top of the altar and clear coals to side and to side and scoop up from the inner, consumed And he would descend and place on the fourth row in the courtyard.
On every day, would scoop up of silver and pour into of gold. But this day, scoops up with of gold, and with he would bring into

On every day, of four kav and pours into three kav. But this day, scoops with three kav, and with it he would bring into Rabbi Yosei says On every day, of a se’a, and pours into three kav. But this day, scoops with three kav, and with it he would bring into
On every day, was heavy. But this day light, On every day, its handle was short, but this day it was long On every day, it was greenish gold, but this day a red the statements of Rabbi Menaḥem.
On every day, sacrificed a peras, in the morning, and a peras in the afternoon, but this day adds handful On every day, was fine but this day it was superfine.

On every day, priests ascend on the eastern side of the ramp and descend on its western side, but this day the High Priest ascends in the middle and descends in the middle. Rabbi Yehuda says: the High Priest always ascends in the middle and descends in the middle,

On every day, the High Priest sanctifies his hands and his feet from the laver and on this day from the golden flask, Rabbi Yehuda says the High Priest always sanctifies his hands and his feet from the golden flask.

On every day there were four arrangements there, but this day there were five; the statement of Rabbi Meir. Rabbi Yosei says: On every day three, but this day four. Rabbi Yehuda says: On every day two, but this day three.
GEMARA: But is it written “And there shall be no man in the Tent of Meeting Rav Yehuda said: teach of the Sanctuary,
The Sages taught “And there shall be no man in the Tent of Meeting
